title: Per-turn status line repaint (CLI only, env-gated)
current_state: Pi paints a 3-line startup banner once (agent/startup_banner.py) and then goes silent on state. Mode, session-cost-so-far, open tickets, L3 row count â€” all invisible after the banner scrolls past. Claude Code's persistent status line is one of the biggest in-the-loop trust signals.
structural_problem: Without continuous state visibility, the user loses track of cost burn, mode drift, and ticket queue depth during long sessions.
target_state: agent/status_line.py exports format_status_line(agent) -> str. PiAgent.run() prints it AFTER each response in CLI mode only, gated by PI_STATUS_LINE=on env. Telegram and voice paths never call it. Format: '[root Â· turn 14 Â· session a3f2e1c Â· $0.038 today Â· 2 open Â· L3: 184]'.
files_affected: pi_agent.py â€” run() loop adds conditional print after response
design_notes: Pure formatter function: takes agent state (mode, turn_number, session_id, daily_cost, open_ticket_count, l3_row_count), returns one-line string.
Wrap data reads in try/except â€” on failure, print '[Pi Â· status unavailable]' rather than crash. Pure observability.
Daily cost: reuse self.evolution.get_daily_cost() â€” already computed for DAILY_COST_LIMIT check.
Open ticket count: glob tickets/open/*.json (cheap, cached at agent level if needed).
L3 row count: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM l3_cache WHERE invalid_at IS NULL. Cheap.
Only fires in CLI run loop. Telegram and voice paths call process_input directly, never see the status line.
Env flag: PI_STATUS_LINE=on. Default off for first week, flip after stability check.
success_criteria: format_status_line(agent) returns valid string for all mode values
PI_STATUS_LINE=on prints status line after every CLI response
PI_STATUS_LINE unset â†’ no print, no overhead
Telegram message round-trip never includes status line (tested via mocked telegram handler)
Status reads that fail (e.g. SQLite locked) fall back to safe placeholder
risk_notes: Low risk. Read-only state, post-response print, isolated to run() loop. Risk: status line appears in CI test stdout and breaks output-parsing tests. Mitigation: default off; opt-in.
migration_plan: 2. Unit-test in isolation with mocked agent dict.
3. In pi_agent.py::run() after the print(response) line, conditionally print status to stderr.
4. Document in README under Env vars.
